site: Vrysinas (peak sanctuary; Rethymnon Museum inv. Λ 4098)
certainty: Printed reading: "AB 10-•[". AB 10 (= U) carries no dots; "•" = trace of a possible second sign (kept out of words); "[" = the text breaks off after it. The "(?)" applies to the ENTIRE classification: "L'inscription peut être classifiée de manière provisoire comme VRY Za 4 (?)" — provisional designation, questioned as a whole. Del Freo further notes the surviving sign "a une forme dépourvue de parallèles dans le corpus du linéaire A", with only "une vague ressemblance" to the AB 10 variants attested on the Mount Iouktas libation tables (cf. IO Za 2b.2 and IO Za 6).
source: Del Freo, Rapport 2016-2021, KO-RO-NO-WE-SA (Ariadne Suppl. 5), p. 108 (sha 40486242...); primary: Tzachili, I. & Georgakopoulos, K. 2015, Ενεπίγραφες μαρτυρίες από το ιερό κορυφής του Βρύσινα, in Αρχαιολογικό Έργο Κρήτης 3 (preliminary publication)
notes: Fragment of a stone libation table [inv. Λ 4098] with one incised sign and possible traces of a second sign to the left of the break; the authors propose the reading AB 10-•[. From the material of Costis Davaras' 1972-73 excavations at the Vrysinas peak sanctuary, kept in the Rethymnon Museum; none of these objects is stratigraphically datable. Recorded despite heavy doubt per instructions: the sign form has no parallel in the Linear A corpus, so U rests only on a vague resemblance to Iouktas libation-table variants of AB 10.
